one_line_takeaway: LASH shows that adaptively re-weighting and semantically re-composing outputs from several existing black-box jailbreak attacks (PAIR, TAP, AutoDAN-Turbo) into one target-specific prompt via a lightweight genetic search substantially outperforms any single attack family on JailbreakBench (84.5%/74.5% ASR1/ASR2 average, +17.7/+33.8pp over the best baseline) using far fewer queries (~30 vs 55-90+), with mechanistic probing suggesting the gain reflects a genuine shift in the target model's internal representations, not just surface rewording.
key_finding: LASH achieves average ASR1 (keyword-based)/ASR2 (two-stage LLM-judge) of 84.50%/74.50% across the six target models with a mean of only 30.01 target queries -- the lowest query cost among iterative baselines. This beats the next-strongest baseline FlipAttack (66.83%/38.83%) and TAP (47.00%/40.67%), i.e. +17.7 and +33.8 percentage points over the strongest baseline on ASR1 and ASR2 respectively (as stated in the paper's own intro). LASH achieves the highest ASR2 in every one of the 10 JailbreakBench harm categories, with the strongest results on Privacy (90.00%), Disinformation (85.00%), Fraud/Deception (83.33%), and Government Decision Making (81.67%), and the weakest (though still leading) on Physical Harm (51.67%) and Sexual/Adult Content (48.33%). Under defenses (ASR2): SmoothLLM 41.50% (vs. next-best AutoDAN-Turbo/TAP at 24.33%/24.83%), Llama Guard 18.00% (vs. AutoDAN-Turbo 18.50%, i.e. roughly tied), perplexity filtering 70.83% (substantially ahead of all baselines, whose scores range roughly 20-41%). Ablations: optimized mixture weights beat uniform weighting, with the largest gain on GPT-4o-mini (ASR2 24% to 85%); LASH beats a 'Seed-Oracle' baseline (succeeds if any individual seed succeeds) on all six models, e.g. Llama-2-7B (80% vs. 49%) and Vicuna-7B (83% vs. 65%), showing the composition itself adds value beyond best-seed selection; GA hyperparameters (G=2, P=8, sigma_mut=0.6) sit in a stable high-performing region -- larger budgets do not consistently help; composition-LLM choice has modest effect (GPT-4o-mini 73.60% vs. Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct 72.00% average ASR2). Mechanistic probing shows LASH induces more 'success-like' hidden-state signatures than baselines, and activation patching shows swapping in LASH's activations moves failed-seed runs toward success-like states (and vice versa), with the strongest effect in intermediate layers.
core_contribution: The paper introduces LASH (LLM Adaptive Semantic Hybridization), a black-box meta-attack for jailbreaking aligned LLMs. Instead of proposing a new single search strategy, LASH treats outputs of multiple existing black-box jailbreak attacks (PAIR, TAP, AutoDAN-Turbo) as a reusable 'seed prompt pool' for a given harmful request, then searches over subsets of seeds and softmax-normalized mixture weights. A composition LLM (GPT-4o-mini) synthesizes a single coherent candidate prompt from the weighted seeds (rather than concatenating text), and a derivative-free genetic algorithm mutates only the real-valued mixture logits based on black-box target-model feedback (never touching the seed prompt text directly). Candidate responses are scored with a two-stage fitness function: a keyword-based refusal filter followed by an LLM-judge score (GPT-4o-mini, threshold tau=9 on a 0-10 scale). The authors also present mechanistic-interpretability analysis (layer-wise logistic probing and bidirectional activation patching) to argue that LASH's gains reflect a genuine shift in the target model's internal representations rather than only surface-level prompt wording.
threat_model: Black-box query-only access to the target model: the attacker can submit arbitrary prompts and observe natural-language responses, but never observes model weights, gradients, or hidden activations -- explicitly framed as reflecting proprietary API-only deployments. This assumption is preserved end to end: all seed-generating base attacks (PAIR, TAP, AutoDAN-Turbo) are themselves black-box methods, and the genetic optimizer relies solely on response-level feedback (keyword refusal detection + LLM-judge score), with no white-box loss, surrogate model, or fine-tuning of any component.
limitations: Authors' own stated limitations: LASH is restricted to single-turn prompt optimization (no multi-turn attack planning) and is not defense-aware (the composition process does not adapt when a defense is present, even though it is evaluated against defenses post hoc). The paper does not evaluate against defenses specifically designed to detect composed/hybridized prompts. The composition and judge LLM used throughout (GPT-4o-mini) is also the same family used as an evaluation judge, which the paper does not discuss as a potential evaluation circularity/bias concern. The 'Mean # Q' comparison for LASH bundles seed-generation queries and GA-search queries, while FlipAttack (non-iterative) and AutoDAN (white-box, uses target-loss evaluations rather than queries) are not directly comparable on this metric, a caveat the paper does flag itself. Broader-impact section explicitly notes dual-use risk and calls for safeguarded release.

datasets_used: JailbreakBench (100 harmful prompts across 10 behavior categories)
quantitative_results: LASH average ASR1/ASR2 = 84.50% / 74.50% across six target models
LASH mean target queries = 30.01 (lowest among iterative baselines)
LASH improves over strongest baseline by +17.7pp (ASR1) and +33.8pp (ASR2)
LASH under SmoothLLM defense: avg ASR2 = 41.50% vs. next-best (AutoDAN-T/TAP) 24.33%/24.83%
LASH under Llama Guard: avg ASR2 = 18.00% vs. AutoDAN-Turbo 18.50%
LASH under perplexity filtering: avg ASR2 = 70.83%, best of all methods

LASH per-category ASR2 highest in all 10 categories: Privacy 90.00%, Disinfo 85.00%, Fraud/Deception 83.33%, Gov. Decision Making 81.67%, Malware/Hacking 80.00%, Expert Advice 78.33%, Harassment/Discrim. 73.33%, Economic Harm 71.67%, Physical Harm 51.67%, Sexual/Adult Content 48.33%
Optimized vs. uniform weights on GPT-4o-mini: ASR2 improves from 24% to 85%
LASH vs. Seed-Oracle: Llama-2-7B 80% vs. 49%, Vicuna-7B 83% vs. 65%
Composition LLM choice: GPT-4o-mini 73.60% avg ASR2 vs. Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct 72.00%
GA config used: population size P=8, mutation scale sigma_mut=0.6, generations G=2
